hypothesis: Enhancing SSTR2 selectivity by introducing aromatic residues at position 5 to better engage SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3 contacts, while introducing hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 to avoid off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid, but the focus must shift to optimizing selectivity by targeting non-pharmacophore positions involved in off-target interactions.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in off-target interactions with SSTR3 and SSTR5. Focusing on these positions will allow us to disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving the critical FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14 pharmacophore.

Introducing charged or hydrophobic mutations at these positions can disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 without affecting the pharmacophore. These mutations are expected to reduce off-target binding and improve Δmargin.
